# RUN 36 — THE PHOTON AS A PARTICLE — ARM A3 (Part-I gauge accommodation + the physical particle ontology)

(run 36, 2026-07-11, Opus 4.8[1m]. Arm A3 of the author-commissioned E2b extension.
Staged: ph_gauge.tex + ph_gauge.md + this ledger. ZERO writes to unification.tex AND ISSUES.md.)

## SCOPE OF THIS ARM (A3 only)
A3 = (1) does the finite-dim Planck-cutoff conditioned-state two-boundary CORE (Part I)
ACCOMMODATE a gauge field (Gauss constraint, transverse physical algebra, BRST/Gupta-Bleuler
on the cutoff)? and (2) THE ONTOLOGY — what IS the photon as a PARTICLE here (helicity +-1
transverse modes, polarization, null dispersion) in the record/decoherence + two-boundary
structure (does it decohere/collapse the same; is a single photon a legitimate record; the
masslessness of the causal-structure carrier). A1 (free-photon first law) and A2 (massless
QEI/Hadamard) are SIBLING arms — I consume their inputs where needed but do not re-adjudicate.

## THE THREE REFUTERS (A3-relevant: (ii) gauge-cutoff kill; (iii) same-wall inherited)
- (ii) GAUGE-CUTOFF KILL: P_{<=E_star}=E_{Hm}([0,E_star]) projecting a gauge field onto a
  finite spectral band breaks gauge invariance / the transverse-longitudinal split, so the
  photon cannot even be PUT in the Part-I core.  -> ADJUDICATED FALSE (see A3-1 below), on the
  precise ground that the cutoff commutes with the Gauss constraint.
- (iii) SAME-WALL KILL (relevant to A1, cross-checked here): the free-photon boost-STRESS edge
  is the SAME A3 wall (gauge current in print, boost STRESS named-not-derived) -> the free
  photon INHERITS the wall, does not escape it.  -> This is an A1 question; A3 finding is
  ORTHOGONAL: the Part-I accommodation does NOT touch the boost-stress edge at all (Part I is
  the finite-dim conditioned-state kinematics, not the n>=1 Rindler descent). So A3 neither
  suffers nor rescues (iii). Flagged for A1.

## #43's PRESUMED HOMES (the honesty targets for THIS arm)
- (a) "free photon = n=0 so the wall is absent" asserted w/o checking the free boost-STRESS
  edge is finite: A3 does NOT make this claim; it is A1's to earn. A3 only uses the FREE n=0
  BRST reduction [Q_BRST,boost]=0 (Kugo-Ojima, run-22 THEOREM-in-model) as the STATEMENT that
  the physical (transverse) subspace is boost/modular-flow-invariant at n=0 — which is what the
  Part-I kinematics needs, and which is genuinely the free result, NOT the interacting descent.
- (b) Maxwell QEI cited beyond proven form: A3 does NOT invoke the QEI (that is A2). A3's
  accommodation is PURELY the finite-dim constraint kinematics; no QEI enters. Flagged: any
  claim that couples A3 to the entropic-gravity FLOOR must route through A2's massless QEI and
  is NOT discharged here.
- (c) transverse-scalar-mechanism transfer assumed clean while masslessness changes the modes:
  THIS is the live A3-adjacent trap. See A3-ONTOLOGY-(c) below — the run-21 mechanism uses ONLY
  the Bose occupation moments <d^2>,<d^3> and the KMS contraction c=2nbar+1, which are
  dispersion-INDEPENDENT (massless and massive bosons share Bose statistics); BUT the polarization
  sum and the transverse projector Pi_{ij}(k)=delta_{ij}-k_i k_j/k^2 are massless-specific (2 pol
  not 3), and the mode functions differ. RE-EARNED for the STATISTICAL/kinematic part; FLAGGED
  as not-A3's-burden for the analytic (QEI/Hadamard) part.

## A3-1 — THE GAUGE-CUTOFF ACCOMMODATION: ADJUDICATION = ACCOMMODATES (finite-dim core carries the transverse photon)  [grade: THEOREM-in-the-finite-model, MODULO one named continuum input]
(details staged in ph_gauge.md sec 2-3; the decisive commutation is [P_{<=E_star}, Gauss]=0)

## A3-1 DETAIL — the gauge-cutoff accommodation
- DECISIVE FACT: [P_{<=E_star}, G(x)] = 0. Reason: P_{<=E_star}=1_{[0,E_star]}(Hm) is a bounded
  Borel function of the GAUGE-INVARIANT physical Hamiltonian Hm=(1/2)int(E^2+B^2); the Gauss
  operator G=div E is conserved and generates gauge, so [Hm,G]=0 => [f(Hm),G]=0 (spectral thm).
  The cutoff maps the constraint surface to itself. Gupta-Bleuler G^{(+)} likewise (Hm preserves
  frequency splitting on the free field).
- REFUTER (ii) conflates "band of the FIELD A_mu" (which would break gauge) with "band of the
  physical Hamiltonian Hm" (which does not). The postulate does the latter (eq:planck-cutoff:
  P=E_{Hm}([0,E_star])). CONFIRMED by reading Pos. planck-cutoff verbatim (l.7344).
- TRANSVERSE SPLIT preserved: split is spatial via Pi_{ij}(k); Hm acts only on transverse sector
  (Coulomb gauge), so P_{<=E_star} is a projection INSIDE the transverse Fock space, no mixing.
- POSITIVE CONSTRUCTION = Prop. fermion (l.2026) with Z_2 grading -> BV ghost-number grading.
  Grade-zero = H^0(s) = gauge-invariant observables = transverse a^T_{k,lambda}. The Part-I
  theorems (born..gen-collapse) are grade-blind (use only positivity/trace/unitarity/POVM), so
  they transport VERBATIM. Not a new theorem; Prop. fermion relabelled. The remark at l.2091

# RUN 36 — ARM A2 — THE MASSLESS QEI / HADAMARD EXTENSION

(run 36, 2026-07-11, Opus 4.8[1m]. Arm A2 of the author-commissioned E2b extension.
Staged: ph_massless.tex (+ .pdf, 4pp, compiles EXIT=0) + ph_massless.py (EXIT=0) + this ledger.
ZERO writes to unification.tex AND ISSUES.md. A1/A3 are sibling arms; consumed, not re-adjudicated.)

## VERDICT (ONE LINE)
The free massless photon does NOT inherit the E2 machine for free. The mass is load-bearing
in EXACTLY ONE of the three legs (L2, the modular constant); the photon adds a fourth,
distinct obstruction (L4, the QEI FORM). The massive window breaks PRECISELY at the
Chialastri-Sanders constant 8E_delta/m and at the spacetime-smeared-vs-worldline QEI-form
mismatch — NOT at the Hadamard-jet Lipschitz estimate. Grade: **massless extension is NOT a
theorem; break precisely localized (NAMED-OPEN at L2, HARD-GAP at L4, PARTIAL rescue at L3).**

## THE FOUR-LEG MAP (each leg's m->0 fate, recomputed)
- **L1 stress-Lipschitz (thm:E2-Lipschitz)** — MASS-INDEPENDENT. L(g0,K)=c[D*C_W+W*C_D+C_Theta]
  is built from Hadamard COINCIDENCE JETS of W_g + curvature polynomials; m appears NOWHERE
  (paper's own proof note l.17531-37: QEI constants do NOT enter L). Survives m->0 as an estimate.
  Photon needs the spin-1 point-splitting op + Maxwell finite part = E2b parametrix (constant-
  tracking, no IR obstruction). **GRADE: transfers modulo Maxwell parametrix; NO massless kill.**
- **L2 modular-energy (eq:E2-CS, Chialastri-Sanders)** — THE load-bearing break. tr|i coth(K_d/2)
  - i coth(K_0/2)| <= 8 E_delta/m; the modular Lipschitz const is 16 C_E/m. RECOMPUTED (PART 3):
  the 1/m = 1/gap of the ultrastatic one-particle h=sqrt(k^2+m^2), inf_k h = m; genuinely diverges
  as gap closes. BUT it is an artifact of the GROUND-STATE-RELATIVE ONE-PARTICLE bound, NOT of the
  modular data: CFM (2312.04629) exhibit the MASSLESS modular Hamiltonian as a bona-fide operator
  (their Eq 3.16, H ~ (l^2-xy)/l * delta'(x-y), finite on [-l,l]); the massive case is a
  perturbation OF it (corrections ~ m ln m -> 0). So the modular OBJECT has a good massless limit;
  it is the PUBLISHED CONSTANT that degenerates. Conformal weight does NOT help (does not remove
  1/gap from a ground-state-relative bound). Replacement massless (boost-based) bound NOT ruled out
  but NOT in print in the ball-uniform spacetime-smeared form, and CFM is 1+1d fermion/interval, not
  3+1d Maxwell. **GRADE: massive window BREAKS here; NAMED-OPEN (plausible replacement, not a theorem).**
- **L3 seam/rate (free-field N1 estimate, ISSUES l.4077-79)** — the "conformal weight rescue" question.
  C_mass = 1/2 + (1/m^2) sup|(xi-1/6)R + v1-curv|; rate (m l)^2 |log m l|. RECOMPUTED (PART 1-2):
  * v0 (Decanini-Folacci) is a POLYNOMIAL in m^2 — FINITE at m=0 for every xi (PART 1). The finite
    part does not blow up; the 1/m^2 lives in the RELATIVE seam constant.
  * ABSOLUTE seam error E_seam = (m l)^2|log m l| * C_mass. Term-by-term m->0:
    (1/2)(m l)^2|log m l| -> 0 ; BUT l^2 Q_curv |log m l| -> +INFINITY.
    **THE TRAP (decisive):** the naive "(m l)^2 * (1/m^2) = l^2 finite so it rescues" is FALSE —
    the POWER cancels but the LOG SURVIVES. Numeric (l=1,Q=1): E_seam = 2.31/6.91/13.82/27.63/55.26
    at m=1e-1/-3/-6/-12/-24 = |log m| EXACTLY. So it is LOGARITHMICALLY IR-divergent unless Q_curv=0.
  * Q_curv=0 iff (xi-1/6)R=0 AND v1-curv=0: holds on FLAT/CONFORMALLY-FLAT (R=0) and (algebraic term)
    at xi=1/6; FAILS for massless MINIMAL xi=0 on curved bg ((0-1/6)R=-R/6, error ~(l^2 R/6)|log m l|).
  **GRADE: conformal weight RESCUES on the flat/conformal locus; softens generic-curved from POWER to
  LOG divergence but does NOT remove it. PARTIAL rescue (this is the honest "does conformal weight save
  the IR integrals" answer: yes on the conformal locus, log-divergent off it).**
- **L4 QEI FORM (Fewster-Pfenning Maxwell QWEI)** — the hardest gap; honesty-home (b). WEB-VERIFIED
  (gr-qc/0303106, JMP 44:4480-4513, 2003; arXiv abstract fetched): the Maxwell/Proca QWEI is
  (i) WORLDLINE (timelike trajectory), NOT 4d-spacetime-smeared; (ii) FIXED-background, NOT ball-uniform;
  (iii) says NOTHING about the conditioned (ABL/2-vector) source floor; (iv) requires Cauchy surface
  COMPACT with TRIVIAL H_1 (=cohomological fingerprint of the Gauss-law/gauge structure, absent from
  the scalar QEI, lifted only for Minkowski). The actual E2a input (Sanders 2024, eq:E2-QEI) is
  SPACETIME-SMEARED + ball-uniform and proved for the SCALAR. NO published spacetime-smeared ball-uniform
  Maxwell QEI exists (lit sweep incl. 2024-25: Maxwell QEIs are worldline-only; spacetime-smeared is
  scalar/Dirac). **GRADE: Maxwell QEI EXISTS at worldline strength but is STRICTLY WEAKER than the E2a
  form; the required FORM is NOT in print for Maxwell. Conflating them = honesty-home (b). HARD GAP.**

## THE run-21 MECHANISM TRANSFER (honesty-home (c), re-earned not assumed) — PART 4
- PER-MODE Bose cancellation A<d^3>+B<d^2>=0 (A=6, B=-(12nbar+6), ratio <d^3>/<d^2>=1+2nbar) is
  IDENTICALLY 0 for EVERY occupation nbar (symbolic) => MASS-BLIND, holds for photon omega=|k| modes.
- BUT nbar(Omega)=1/(e^{2pi Omega}-1) ~ 1/(2pi Omega) as boost freq Omega->0 (gapless photon). The
  covariance-weight integrand rho0 nbar(1+nbar) ~ rho0/(4pi^2 Omega^2) is IR-DIVERGENT; massive field
  floors Omega by the rest energy => finite. So assembled O^(1) = -beta INT dOmega rho [A<d^3>+B<d^2>]
  = INT(0) = 0 FORMALLY, but as a cancellation BETWEEN TWO IR-DIVERGENT integrals (0 * inf).
- **GRADE: per-mode cancellation TRANSFERS; assembled free-photon O^(1)=0 is an IR-REGULATED inf-inf
  identity — PLAUSIBLE (integrand identically 0 pre-integration; symmetric IR cutoff preserves it) but
  must be RE-EARNED with an explicit IR regulator, NOT asserted from run-21 (which used gapped modes).**

## THE THREE REFUTERS (A2-adjudicated)
- **(i) massless-QEI kill: PARTIALLY UPHELD.** The E2a/Hadamard machinery does NOT uniformly need the
  mass gap (L1 mass-free; L3 conformally rescued on flat/conformal locus). BUT the load-bearing modular
  constant (L2) degenerates AND no Maxwell QEI of the required (spacetime-smeared, ball-uniform,
  conditioned-source) form exists (L4). The kill lands on L2+L4, precisely localized — NOT on the
  Hadamard estimate. Net: massless extension is NOT a theorem.
- **(ii) gauge-cutoff kill: A3's scope.** A2 adds independent evidence: the H_1=0 topological
  restriction on the Maxwell QEI (Prop FP) is the gauge/cohomology structure obstructing the machinery.
- **(iii) same-wall kill: A1/A3's scope.** A2 adds: even the FREE photon's QEI/Hadamard leg carries new
  content (L2, L4), so "free => trivial" fails already at the QEI level, independent of the boost-stress edge.

## HONESTY (#43 homes hunted this arm)
- (a) "free photon = n=0 so wall absent": A1/A3 scope; not asserted in A2.
- (b) "Maxwell QEI cited beyond proven form": **GUARDED** — Prop FP exhibits the exact form gap
  (worldline / fixed-metric / H_1=0 / no conditioned floor) and refuses the conflation. Web-verified.
- (c) "transverse-scalar transfer clean while masslessness changes modes": **GUARDED** — Prop mechanism:

- No massive-window smuggling; no fitted factors; no positivity resurrection; ceiling formal-pAQFT.

## SCOPE (A1 only)

grade by reducing to the run-22 free BRST reduction (Kugo-Ojima quartet cancellation, exact Krein
<N_ghost>=-n_bose) + the transverse scalar mechanism? (2) Is the free boost-STRESS edge term FINITE
(wall ABSENT) or the SAME A3 object (#43 home (a) — CHECK, do not assume)? Deliver the free-photon
first-law status + EXACTLY which run-22 results transfer. A2 (massless QEI/Hadamard) and A3 (Part-I
gauge accommodation + ontology) are SIBLING arms.

## THE DECISIVE LOGICAL SEPARATION (two objects, kept apart)
- (O1) the n=0 FIRST LAW: dS_rel = d<K_W^(0)> = 2pi int_B x^1 d<T00^(0)>. K_W^(0)=2pi int x^1 T00^(0)
  by BISOGNANO-WICHMANN (geometric, mass-independent, holds m=0). A ONE-POINT, FIRST-VARIATION
  statement. NO descent current, NO IBP.
- (O2) the A3 WALL (runs 24-25): the edge term [x^1 Y^1]_{x^1=0} of the modular-moment IBP
  s.T00^int = d_1 Y^1 + d_perp Y^perp of the INTERACTING boost-BRST descent current Y^mu. A
  TWO-POINT NORM x^{1 2}<Y^dag Y> of a DIM-3 O(g) current, on the rho^{-4} dimensional floor.
  Exists ONLY to prove [Q_int,K_W^int]=0 at n>=1.

## VERDICT (per piece)

### (i) THE WALL — ABSENT at n=0. Refuter (iii) "same-wall kill" DEFEATED.
At n=0: NO interaction, NO T00^int, NO descent Y^mu, NO IBP, hence NO edge term. [Q_0,K_W^(0)]=0 is
DIRECT (=beta*[Q0,H_free]=0 by BW), not via a descent. The #43-home-(a) trap ("wall absent asserted
w/o checking") is CONFRONTED, not assumed:
  - the free boost-STRESS edge term is checked ABSENT (eq (*) is vacuous at n=0 — no O(g) source);
  - the one-point Minkowski-referenced moment int x^1 <T00>_Mink-ref is checked FINITE (=0 identically,
    since the n=0 wedge KMS state IS the Minkowski vacuum by BW; the run-25 rho^{-2}/rho^{-4} powers are
    BOULWARE-referenced — the WRONG reference for the n=0 law);
  - INDEPENDENT LITERATURE CONFIRMATION (Bostelmann-Cadamuro-Del Vecchio 2012.14401, CMP 389:661): for
    coherent-state relative entropy on general CCR algebras, the FIRST derivative (= the first law) is a
    BULK Cauchy-surface integral with NO boundary term; the EDGE term (integral over the wedge edge
    x^1=x^0) appears ONLY in the SECOND derivative (QNEC/convexity). So the free-field first law is
    bulk-only at first order; the edge object is a 2nd-order structure — exactly where the framework's
    interacting wall lives.
  The free photon does NOT inherit the A3 wall. GRADE: THEOREM (wall absent at n=0).

### (ii) THE RUN-22/23 BRST REDUCTION — TRANSFERS to Maxwell, THEOREM-in-model.
Reproduced from scratch (ph_freelaw.py, finite Maxwell-quartet Fock/Krein KMS model, dim=144:
transverse +-1 physical; L,S,c,cbar unphysical; beta=2pi, w=0.5):
  ||Q0^2||=0 (nilpotent); ||[Q0,H]||=7.7e-15 (KO boost/KMS anchor); gh#(Q0)=+1 exact;
  STr(e^{-bH}{Q0,X})=2.6e-17 (Kugo-Ojima super-trace, 8 random X);
  EXACT KREIN: <Nc>_STr=-0.045166, <NL>_STr=+0.045166=n_bose => <N_ghost>_STr=-n_bose EXACTLY; Z_phys=1;
  Maxwell quartet cancellation <T00_unphys>_STr(L+S+c+cbar)=-3.9e-8 ~ 0.
Matches run-23 ground truth (+-0.045166, Z_phys=1) to precision with the MAXWELL sector labels.
GRADE: THEOREM-in-model. (Same ceiling as run-23 — finite model / calibration — BUT for the FREE
photon there is NO horizon-locality gate to close [(i)], so the in-model quartet cancellation IS the
whole unphysical-sector n=0 story.)

### (iii) THE FREE-PHOTON FIRST LAW ITSELF — PLAUSIBLE, gated by the E2b Maxwell transfer.
BW (K_W^(0)=2pi int x^1 T00^(0)) is kinematic and holds for m=0 EM. BUT the coherent-state
relative-entropy FIRST LAW dS_rel=d<K_W^(0)> is IN PRINT for the free SCALAR only (web-verified):
  - Longo 1901.02366 "Entropy of coherent excitations": free NEUTRAL (scalar) QFT, any dim.

  - Casini-Grillo-Pontello 1903.00109: free SCALAR, Araki formula; flags the stress-tensor

    from single-particle modular data; requires "differential modular position" regularity. Examples:

So the 4d Maxwell instance (gauge/Gauss structure, transverse two-point functions, differential-
modular-position + improving-term conditions verified) is the E2b named-open transfer. NOT a wall: it
reduces to assembling the Longo/CLR/BCDV apparatus for the free Maxwell net (writable, NO operator

### (#43 home (c)) THE TRANSVERSE-MECHANISM TRANSFER — RE-EARNED non-load-bearing at n=0.
The run-21 transverse mechanism (thermal-Wick :A^4: diagonal orthogonal to linear K_W by the Bose
ratio) is the INTERACTING n=1 orthogonality O^(1)=0, which used MASSIVE Bogoliubov modes. The n=0
first law does NOT use it — it uses only BW + Longo/CLR (mass-independent, no Bogoliubov mode

### (1) HOME (b) — FEWSTER-PFENNING AT ACTUAL STRENGTH — verified against the PRIMARY source
The ARM's characterization (ph_massless.tex Prop:FP, ph_refute) is FACTUALLY CORRECT, checked against arXiv:gr-qc/0303106 (abstract + WebFetch):
  - "A Quantum Weak Energy Inequality for SPIN-ONE Fields in Curved Spacetime", Fewster-Pfenning, JMP 44(10):4480-4513 (2003). Citation EXACT.
  - WORLDLINE-averaged ("along an arbitrary timelike trajectory"), NOT spacetime-smeared. CONFIRMED by primary source; "no spacetime-smeared or open-region-averaged variant mentioned."

The chain is (transverse photon net):
  (S1) BW:  K_W^(0) = -log Delta_{omega_0} = 2pi K_boost = 2pi int_Sigma x^1 :T00: dSigma
       (geometric, field-general, m=0-valid).  [import: Bisognano-Wichmann]

       datum built from GAUGE-INVARIANT field data (F_{munu} / transverse A^T);
       the CCR/Weyl coherent state of the transverse field.
  (S3) BCDV Thm 2.13 (hypothesis-FREE):  S_rel(omega_A || omega_0)|_{A(W)} = S_{L(W)}(a),
       the single-particle entropy form of the transverse standard subspace L(W).
  (S4) Longo/CLR mean-value identity:  S_rel = <K_W^(0)>_{omega_A} - <K_W^(0)>_{omega_0}
       = Delta<K_W^(0)>  (the coherent state shifts the modular energy by the classical
       boost energy of the excitation; the entropy term cancels for coherent states).
  (S5) first variation along the boost/parameter flow lambda -> a_lambda:
       dS_rel = d<K_W^(0)> = 2pi int_Sigma x^1 d<T00>_{omega_A}.  QED-modulo-imports.

THE DECISIVE STRUCTURAL POINT (independently reached by the R-refuter above):
  The differential-modular-position hypothesis (BCDV Def 3.2) is the MAIN ASSUMPTION
  of BCDV Sec 3 = the SECOND-derivative/QNEC analysis ONLY. The FIRST-law engine is
  BCDV Thm 2.13 (l.669-701), which is HYPOTHESIS-FREE (needs only the standing CCR +
  quasifree/standardness assumptions). Hence:

  Run-36 named that hypothesis as a gate on the first law; that was over-conservative.

    mechanism for refuter (ii).

    HYPOTHESIS-FREE (scalar + conformal U(1)-current worked; NOT 4d Maxwell).

THE THREE REFUTER AXES — how A1 handles them (see mx_skeleton.tex Sec on refuters):
  (i)  BCDV-hypothesis-fails kill: DEFANGED at the SKELETON level — the hypothesis is not
       used by the first law. (ARM B still verifies it FOR the QNEC/second-deriv completeness;
       A1 does not need B's verdict for the first-law grade.)
  (ii) coherent-state-gauge kill: S_rel is gauge-invariant because (a) BCDV/Araki define it
       state-theoretically (unambiguous, CGP), and (b) the coherent-state LABEL redundancy
       a ~ a + (gauge) lands in L(W)' (the symplectic complement = pure-gauge/longitudinal),
       which the entropy form S_L annihilates (BCDV Lemma 2.12(iv): ker S_L = L'). So the
       gauge orbit of the label maps to a SINGLE value of S_rel. VERIFIED numerically.
  (iii) m=0-IR kill: S_rel is IR-FINITE at m=0 even though per-mode occupations n_k ~ 1/omega
       are not summable. S_rel = S_L(a) is a MODULAR-energy-weighted norm (the boost weight
       x^1 and the modular c(K)^2 form soften the IR); the finiteness criterion is
       a in dom(S_L) <=> the boost energy 2pi int x^1 T00[a] < infinity, an IR-SAFE condition
       on smooth compactly-supported classical data. Exhibited numerically (IR-regulated ->
       limit finite) below.

(sub-piece log appended per turn.)

## ARM A3 — THE STRESS TENSOR + GAUGE REDUCTION (Opus 4.8, 2026-07-11)
================================================================================
Role: the RIGHT-HAND SIDE of the first law + its BRST grade-zero reduction.
Staged: mx_stress.tex (5pp, compiles clean) + mx_stress.py (all blocks PASS).
Deliverable = a gauge-invariant, improvement-UNIQUE, BRST grade-zero RHS
  2pi int_Sigma x^1 T00 = 2pi K_boost = -log Delta (BW),  T00 = 1/2(E^2+B^2).
A3 does NOT prove the LHS equality dS_rel = d<K> (that is A1 skeleton + B's BCDV
hypothesis + the scalar imports). NO writes to unification.tex / ISSUES.md.

--- A3.1  T00 gauge-invariance + IMPROVING-TERM UNIQUENESS (the crux, #44 home (a)) ---
GRADE: THEOREM (symbolically verified in mx_stress.py blocks 1-2b).
  * T_{mu nu}=F_{mu a}F_nu^a - 1/4 eta F^2:  symmetric, traceless (T^mu_mu=0),
    conserved on-shell (d_mu T^{mu nu}=F^{nu}_lam d_mu F^{mu lam}=0), and
    T00 = 1/2(E^2+B^2). All PASS symbolically.
  * THE DECISIVE NEW CONTENT (verified for Maxwell, NOT transferred from the scalar):
    the scalar (CGP 1903.00109 eq 1.10-1.12) has a 1-parameter improving family
    (lambda/2pi)(d_mu d_nu - eta box):phi^2: that deposits a NONZERO edge term
    lambda int_{x1=0}:phi^2: in K_R; CGP prove lambda=0 by rel-entropy
    positivity+monotonicity (their eq 2.5). FOR MAXWELL: the CGP edge operator is a
    gauge-invariant local SCALAR of dimension d-2=2. There is NO such operator:
      dim 2: NONE (A_mu A^mu is dim 2 but NOT gauge-invariant; delta(A^2)!=0 shown);
      dim 4: F^2, F Ftilde (F itself is dim 2 per antisym pair).
    Lowest gauge-invariant local scalar = F^2 at DIM 4. => no dim-2 edge scalar =>
    no lambda-tunable boundary term => the boost moment is IMPROVEMENT-UNIQUE.
  * BOTH improvement channels close (mx_stress.py block 2b):
      (trace/CGP)  Delta T00=(d_mu d_nu - eta box)Theta = 3-Laplacian(Theta) on the
                   slice; Delta K = 2pi int x^1 (3-Lap Theta) IBP-> edge functional of
                   Theta|_{x1=0}, needs a dim-2 gauge-inv scalar => NONE (const Theta
                   gives 3-Lap=0);
      (Belinfante) T -> T + d_alpha Psi^alpha_{(mu nu)}, same double-IBP -> same dim-2
                   gauge-inv edge scalar => NONE.
    => Delta K = 0 identically. Maxwell is STRUCTURALLY BETTER than the scalar: the
    gauge structure REMOVES the ambiguity CGP had to eliminate; no positivity argument
    needed. This closes #44 home (a) on the stress side (verified, not transferred).
  * Reconciliation: the would-be improving operators (A^2, (d.A)^2, cbar c) are exactly
    the ghost/gauge-fixing BRST-exact fields — they live in the sector the quartet
    mechanism (A3.3) annihilates. No tension with "improvement-unique".

--- A3.2  BW: K_W^(0) = 2pi K_boost = 2pi int x^1 T00 ---
GRADE: THEOREM (in-print import), stated at proven strength.
  * Bisognano-Wichmann is field-general (any finite-component Wightman field; realized
    for Maxwell/free-net nets), geometric-boost content MASS-INDEPENDENT. -log Delta =
    2pi K_boost, and the boost GENERATOR = moment of the improvement-unique T00 (A3.1).
  * SCOPE (honesty): BW-for-scalar = CGP 1.5; field-general geometric boost = BW
    in-print; boost generator = 2pi int x^1 T00[gauge-inv Maxwell] = classical Noether +
    A3.1 uniqueness. The REL-ENTROPY first law using this K is NOT A3 and NOT in-print
    for 4d Maxwell (A1/B, the NEW run-37 content).
  * Sanity (block 3): transverse massless mode has |E|=|B| (null, traceless), time-avg
    density = omega^2/2 > 0 => 2pi int x^1 T00_T is a genuine POSITIVE boost energy.

--- A3.3  BRST reduction: quartet drops from BOTH sides at n=0 ---
GRADE: THEOREM-in-model (finite Kugo-Ojima KMS model, run-22/23 reproduced to machine
precision in mx_stress.py block 4).
  * KREIN IDENTITY reproduced INDEPENDENTLY at beta=2pi, w=0.5:
      <N_L>=<N_S>=+0.045166,  <N_c>=<N_cbar>=-0.045166  [nbar(0.5,2pi)=0.04516571 exact],
      <N_ghost>_STr = N_c+N_cbar = -0.090332 = -(N_L+N_S) = -n_bose   (EXACT, |diff|<1e-12).
  * <T00_quartet>_phys = w*(N_L+N_S+N_c+N_cbar) = 0 (0.0e0); d<T00_quartet> = 0 (0.0e0)
    => quartet contributes ZERO to <T00> AND to its first variation => drops from RHS.
  * Z_phys = [(1/(1-e^-bw))*(1-e^-bw)]^2 = 1.0000000000 (genuine cohomology, not
    coincidental zero); STr(e^-bH {Q,X})=0 by (-1)^F grading of an FP-odd operator (KO).
  * LHS: physical coherent state displaced by BRST-CLOSED transverse data; quartet

--- A3's BEARING ON THE REFUTER (RHS only; refuter arm owns the full verdict) ---

--- A3 NET ---
A3.1 THEOREM (incl. the improvement-uniqueness, verified for Maxwell / #44-(a) closed on

  G2: even the SECOND-deriv condition HOLDS for the transverse wedge via BCDV Lemma 3.3(c)
      (L0<L1 factorial + U(x)L0<L0, x>=0). The transverse null-translated wedge family = a
      HALF-SIDED MODULAR INCLUSION (BCDV Sec4 Prop4.4). BCDV list "lightlike translated wedge
      algebras in the free massive field [CLR19]" as verified (Sec3.1). Transverse/longitudinal
      split does NOT enter — constrained modes removed by BRST before any modular datum.

KILL (ii) — "coherent-state gauge label spoils S_rel": DEFEATED; gauge-invariance CONFIRMED.
  (A) S_rel is a functional of the STATES on the gauge-invariant transverse algebra, not the label.
  (B) ker S_L = L' (BCDV Lemma 2.12(iv), l.641). Pure-gauge displacement = symplectically null in L'
      => S_L(a_tr+a_gauge)=S_L(Q_L a)=S_L(a_tr). Gauge orbit -> one point.
  (C) Improving-term ambiguity on the RHS (edge term on the half-space) FIXED by CGP 1903.00109
      Sec2 (eqs 2.3-2.5): positivity=>lambda>=0, monotonicity=>lambda<=0, so lambda=0. Canonical
      stress unique. State-theoretic => transfers to transverse Maxwell. T00=(1/2)(E^2+B^2) already GI.
  NUM (kill_ii): gauge-orbit deviation EXACTLY 0.

KILL (iii) — "IR divergence sneaks into S_rel at m=0": DEFEATED; IR-finiteness CONFIRMED.
  <N>=int d^3k|a|^2/(2omega) IS log-IR-divergent at m=0 for soft tail a~1/omega (soft-photon thm) — real.
  But S_rel = S_L(a) = 2pi int x^1 (1/2)(E^2+B^2) = modular BOOST ENERGY of a FIXED smooth field:
  local GI energy density, mode weight ~omega NOT ~1/omega => IR soft-SUPPRESSED. CLR criterion
  (1906.01707 ~eq12): S_k finite iff -int log(l) d(k,E(l)k)<inf, a spectral condition on ONE Schwartz
  vector, not a mode sum; CLR state setup for (box+m^2)Phi=0 and note "in particular ... m=0" (l.111).
  NUM (kill_iii): same soft datum: <N>~log(1/kmin) slope -6.29 (divergent); energy proxy E[a]->2pi plateau
  (spread 1e-1@kmin=1e-1 down to <1e-4@kmin=1e-6), identical under massive regulator m->0. E[a] is a
  rigorous UPPER proxy (boost weight x^1 bounded on compact support, ->0 at horizon); NOT claimed E[a]=S_rel.

RESIDUAL (same for all three): the 4d-MAXWELL INSTANCE of (M1) transverse-Hadamard=>BW modular data

VERDICT: the differential-modular-position / regularity condition HOLDS for the
4d free transverse Maxwell field. NO hypothesis fails.

WHY IT IS VERIFICATION, NOT TRANSFER (the #44 home (a) trap avoided):
  BCDV do NOT check Def 3.2 field-by-field. Sec 4 is an ABSTRACT reduction:
    Prop 4.9 (l.1534): every -HSMI of standard subspaces {V(theta(t))H} is a
      family of differential modular inclusions.
    Prop 4.8 (l.1526): every -HSMI = direct sum of copies of the UNIQUE
      irreducible U(1) one + a trivial part.
    Lemma 4.1(c) (l.1200): direct sums of families of diff-mod-inclusions are ones.
  => the condition is decided ENTIRELY by whether the transverse photon wedge
     one-particle net is a -HSMI of standard subspaces. That reduces (via
     Borchers' commutation thm, single-particle form) to four free-field facts:
       (H1) STANDARD:   L^T(W) standard in K^T (Reeh-Schlieder for F_{munu}).
       (H2) BW-GEOM:    Delta^{-it} = geometric boost (BW for the covariant
                        Wightman field F_{munu}; field-general, m=0-VALID).
       (H3) HSMI:       positive light-cone translation P_+ = k^0-k^1 >= 0 on the
                        massless shell, wedge-preserving for a>=0.
       (H3') FACTORIAL: L^T(W) type III_1 factor (free-EM wedge algebra).
  Each (H1)-(H3') is a property of F_{munu} / the massless helicity-+-1 Wigner
  rep, established AT THE LEVEL OF THE FIELD, never by invoking the scalar. The
  scalar and the photon are PARALLEL INSTANCES of the same abstract reduction.

THE DECISIVE SCOPING CORRECTION (load-bearing honesty; agrees w/ Arm A1 + R-refuter):
  The differential-modular-position condition is the hypothesis of BCDV's
  SECOND-derivative / QNEC analysis (Sec 3), NOT of the FIRST law. The run-37
  first law dS_rel = d<K_W^(0)> = 2pi int x^1 d<T00> rests on BCDV Thm 2.13 /
  Prop 2.10 (HYPOTHESIS-FREE: any symplectic Hilbert space, any quasifree state,
  any closed subspace; only standing assumption = dim ker D even/infinite,
  automatic in QFT) + Bisognano-Wichmann (K_L = 2pi boost). So:
    - the FIRST law is THEOREM-modulo-(Thm 2.13, BW) [does NOT need the condition];
    - the QNEC-strength apparatus is ADDITIONALLY available because the condition
      ALSO holds (Thm B), and even its C^1 smoothness refinement is met (BCDV
      eq 5.9-5.10 per helicity summand: T_f is C^1).
  Run-36 gated the first law on this condition; that was over-conservative. The
  real gate is the FIELD-CONTENT check (H1)-(H3'), which passes.

THE GAUGE STRUCTURE (the exact scalar/Maxwell difference, checked NOT assumed):
  The transverse-longitudinal split / Gauss constraint does NOT obstruct, because
  it is discharged BEFORE the modular theory: K^T is the massless helicity-+-1
  Wigner rep (transversality k.eps=0 IS the solved Gauss constraint), an ordinary
  positive-definite Hilbert space on which the boost and P_+ act unitarily and
  geometrically. The longitudinal/temporal directions are simply ABSENT from K^T;
  there is no operator constraint left to spoil Delta or Borchers' relations.

REFUTER HOMES, ALL CLOSED (numerically exhibited, mx_bcdv.py 13/13):
  (i)  transverse-longitudinal/Gauss obstruction: NO. CHECK 1 shows the diff-mod-
       position mechanism is helicity-MULTIPLICITY-BLIND (verified on the C^2
       two-polarization spectral family) — Prop 4.9's direct-sum structure needs
       nothing scalar-specific. CHECK 2: Borchers Delta^{it}U(a)Delta^{-it} =
       U(e^{-2pi t}a) holds on the 1-particle wedge (rel err 1.7e-4).
  (ii) coherent-state gauge: S_rel gauge-invariant. It is a function of the
       one-particle image a in K^T (factors through F_{munu}); pure-gauge shifts
       have F=0 -> same a. And ker S_L = L' (BCDV Prop 2.12(v)) kills any residual
       longitudinal label. Consistent with CGP (Araki S_rel state-theoretic,
       unambiguous). Single number on the gauge orbit.
  (iii) m=0 IR: S_rel IR-FINITE. S_rel = 2pi int x^1 T00 = horizon-distance-
       weighted boost energy, finite on compact smooth data with NO regulator and
       NO 1/m. CHECK 3 (decisive contrast at m=0, kmin->0): total occupation
       Ntot DIVERGES 4.3 -> 62511 (soft-photon), while modular energy Smod stays
       pinned at 5.56833 (IR-safe). DIFFERENT objects: S_rel is not sum_k n_k.
       Contrast the SEPARATE run-36 A2 axis eq:E2-CS 8E_delta/m (a spectral-gap
       artifact of a DIFFERENT object, non-coherent Gaussian modular difference)
       which DOES diverge; that is NOT this law. This law is mass-independent.

GRADE: the BCDV hypothesis for 4d Maxwell = VERIFIED, THEOREM-modulo-named-free-

ARM B — CITATION SHARPENING (web-verified, #44 home (b) guard):
  (H2) BW-for-Maxwell has a PRECISE in-print home, and it is NOT the scalar:
    Dybalski-Morinelli, "The Bisognano-Wichmann property for asymptotically
    complete massless QFT", arXiv:1909.12809, CMP 377 (2020): BW holds for
    asymp-complete massless Haag-Kastler theories whose particles are scalar OR
    a DIRECT SUM OF TWO OPPOSITE INTEGER HELICITIES -- "thus, e.g., PHOTONS ARE
    COVERED." The finite-helicity MODULARITY CONDITION (Morinelli 1604.04750 /
    1703.06831) is PROVEN for finite-helicity reps + multiplicities (Buchholz
    massless scattering). Free Maxwell = asymp-complete, massless, helicity +-1
    = opposite-helicity direct sum. => BW for the photon wedge is a THEOREM.
  GUARD: the scalar-only example in 1703.06831 abstract ("direct integrals of
    scalar massive and massless reps") must NOT be cited AS the photon result;
    the photon result is the separate 1909.12809. Corrected in mx_bcdv.tex (H2).
  (H2 route i) BW 1975/76 for finite-component covariant Wightman fields also
    applies to F_{munu}, but the MASSLESS finite-helicity subtlety (indefinite
    potential; work with gauge-invariant F) is exactly what 1909.12809 discharges
    -- so route (ii) is the honest primary citation.

relative-entropy first law dS_rel = d<K_W^(0)> = 2pi int x^1 d<T00> on the

conflict (refuter = NEGATIVE-RESULT converging with A1/B; its residual (M1)+(M2) = A1
Hyp 1 = B (H1)-(H3')).

INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED against primary sources (not taken on the arms' word):
1. BCDV Thm 2.13 (bcdv.txt l.669-701) is HYPOTHESIS-FREE: proof uses only Sec-2
   standing setup (standard subspace, quasifree state, ker D parity) + CLR Thm 4.5
   approximation. Differential-modular-position (Def 3.2, l.782-803) is the Sec-3
   SECOND-derivative/QNEC condition. => the FIRST law does NOT need it. The run-36
   gating was over-conservative; correction upheld. [#44 home (a): decisive scoping.]
2. Abstract reduction Prop 4.9 (l.1534) = Prop 4.8 (l.1526) + Prop 4.4 + Lemma 4.1(c)
   (l.1200): every -HSMI of standard subspaces => family of differential modular
   inclusions, field-content-blind up to irreducible multiplicity. Two-helicity photon
   = (irreducible U(1) HSMI)^{oplus 2}, handled by Lemma 4.1(c). [crux: sound.]
3. BW-for-photon: Dybalski-Morinelli 1909.12809 abstract WEB-VERIFIED "photons are
   covered" (asympt. complete massless, direct sum of opposite integer helicities).
   Free Maxwell trivially asympt. complete. [#44 home (b): in print, NOT the scalar.
   MINOR CITATION FIX: arm mx_bcdv.tex says "CMP 377 (2020)"; actual = CMP 380,
   pp.1267-1294 (2020). Cosmetic; result content correct.]
4. S_rel = 2pi int x^1 T00 is an EQUALITY (CGP eq 1.5 K_Omega=2pi int x^1 T00, eq 1.6
   S_rel=Delta<K>-DeltaS, DeltaS=0 for coherent states), NOT a bound. [refuter's
   upper-bound framing is MORE conservative than necessary but not wrong.]
5. ker S_L = L' (BCDV Lem 2.12(iv), l.641/655-663). Pure-gauge label has F=0 => zero
   one-particle image, symplectically null, annihilated. Plus state-theoretic Araki.
   [refuter (ii) gauge-invariance: CONFIRMED both ways.]
6. Improvement-uniqueness for Maxwell (A3.1, the one genuinely-new RHS claim):
   REFEREE TRIED TO CONSTRUCT A COUNTEREXAMPLE AND FAILED. No gauge-invariant dim-2
   Lorentz scalar exists (eta^{mu nu}F_{mu nu}=0; single F is a tensor; A^2 not
   gauge-inv; F^2 is dim 4). Both improvement channels (CGP-trace + Belinfante/
   superpotential) require the same nonexistent dim-2 edge scalar => DeltaK=0. Maxwell
   is STRUCTURALLY BETTER than the scalar (gauge symmetry removes the ambiguity CGP
   had to kill by positivity). CONFIRMED. [referee_probe.py PROBE 1]
7. THE CRUX (#44 home a): "is the single-particle modular flow genuinely smooth for
   the transverse field?" YES. Referee's independent Borchers-commutation probe first
   FAILED (rel err 1.86) on a self-inflicted dilation-DIRECTION convention bug;
   corrected (matching BCDV Thm 4.6 V(delta(2pi s))=Delta^{+is}), Borchers'
   Delta^it U(a) Delta^-it = U(e^-2pi t a) is an EXACT ANALYTIC operator identity per
   helicity (0.00e+00), two helicities UNMIXED under wedge boost (off-diag overlap 0),
   P_+=p>=0 positive-energy semigroup. Flow is smooth; -HSMI holds. CONFIRMED.
   [referee_probe.py PROBE 2 + follow-up analytic check. The arm's mx_bcdv.py 1.7e-4
   was discretized interpolation error, reproduced.]
8. EVERY IMPORT AT PROVEN FIELD CONTENT (#44 home b):
   - CLR 1906.01707 = Klein-Gordon SCALAR (l.14/107/522), EXPLICITLY admits m=0
     (l.111 "in particular a solution of the wave equation when m=0"). Not EM.
   - CGP 1903.00109 = free hermitian SCALAR (l.415), improving-term killed by
     positivity+monotonicity lambda=0 (l.194-196/254-255). Not EM.

   - BCDV 2012.14401 = general CCR; scalar + conformal U(1)-current worked, NOT 4d

   - BW = geometric/field-general/mass-independent.
   The 4d-Maxwell coherent-state-rel-entropy INSTANCE is correctly flagged NEW
   everywhere, never cited as in-print. [#44 homes (a),(b),(c) all clean.]
9. #44 home (c) m=0 IR: S_rel=2pi int x^1 T00[A] IR-finite for smooth compact data
   (smooth compact non-neg integrand); soft-photon NUMBER int|a|^2/2|k| diverges but

  run-36 PLAUSIBLE) SURVIVES adversarial audit. No overclaim #44 of the

WHAT SURVIVED EACH ATTACK:
 [A1] The scoping correction (first law = hypothesis-free Thm 2.13 + BW,
   NOT differential-modular-position) is LEGITIMATE, not a dodge. Verified:
   the run-37 theorem (mx_skeleton.tex eq:thm) differentiates along the
   STATE flow lambda->a_lambda at FIXED wedge W, NOT the region/wedge-
   translation flow. Because S_rel = S_L(a) = 2pi<a,K_boost a> is a STATIC
   exact identity (Thm 2.13, bcdv l.669-701, hypothesis-free; + CGP eq 5.12
   = 2pi<f_R,k_1 f_R>, 1903 l.1009; + BW), d/dlambda is elementary. BCDV's
   Def 3.2 is needed only to differentiate w.r.t. the REGION family L_t
   (Sec 3.3 opens "for a family of differential modular inclusions",
   bcdv l.952). The state-flow first law genuinely does not touch it.
 [S4] S_rel(coherent) = Delta<K> with NO separate DeltaS is EXACTLY CGP
   Thm 5.9/eq 5.12 at single-particle level — verified against 1903 l.1009-
   1023 (SCALAR, cited correctly as scalar). BCDV Prop 2.10 (eq 2.28) gives
   S_rel = sigma(g, i K_L g) as an EQUALITY (not bound); the "<g,K_L g>-type"
   phrasing in mx_bcdv.tex l.68 is faithful (hedged), no hidden gap.
 [B] Differential-modular-position DOES additionally hold for Maxwell via

   (H1)-(H3') are in-print free-field facts. BW-for-photon = Dybalski-

   "scalar or a direct sum of two opposite integer helicities, thus, e.g.,

 [A3.1] Improvement-uniqueness "Maxwell better than scalar" (no dim-2

THE ONE PRECISION FLAG (strengthens the honest residual; does NOT refute):
  The PRE-EXISTING unification.tex E2b sentence (l.17675, a run-36-era
  prose name-drop, NOT a \cite, no bib entry for 2012.14401) reads:
  "the free first law is bulk (no wedge-edge boundary term at first
  variation --- Bostelmann-Cadamuro-Del Vecchio)". Adversarial reading:
  BCDV EXPLICITLY warn (bcdv l.128-132) that the clean "first derivative =
  bulk only, second = boundary" split "does not hold up in general" and is
  replaced by a two-sided SECOND-derivative estimate (Thm 3.9 l.1096). The
  clean bulk-first-derivative is CLR's SCALAR computation (Thm 3.6,
  1906 l.823-832, by direct IBP + continuity eq). For the PHOTON, "no edge
  term at first variation" is true ONLY via the HSMI/geometric structure =
  exactly the NEW run-37 Arm-B verification. So attributing that photon
  property to "BCDV" uses BCDV for the general bulk-vs-boundary STRUCTURE
  (legitimate) rather than for a Maxwell theorem BCDV proved (they did not).
  ADJUDICATION: NOT a GAP-FOUND, because (i) the sentence is hedged at
  "theorem-in-model grade" with "transfers" language (instance work, not
  in-print completion); (ii) it cites the general dichotomy, whose scalar/
  U(1) instance IS in-print, applied to argue the wall (a 2nd-variation
  edge object) is absent at 1st variation — logically sound; (iii) the
  underlying photon claim is TRUE (run-37 verified the HSMI). It is a
  citation-PRECISION nuance, best sharpened to "(CLR scalar prototype;
  BCDV general-CCR abstraction; the 4d-Maxwell bulk first-variation is the
  run-37 HSMI instance)". Non-load-bearing for the run-37 grade, which
  never rests on that sentence.

=== (1) THE FIRST LAW: VERDICT = THEOREM-modulo-named-in-print-imports ===

GRADE: THEOREM (modulo the named in-print scalar/CCR imports (I0)-(I5)).
This UPGRADES the run-36 free-photon first law from PLAUSIBLE to
THEOREM-modulo-in-print-imports.

I REPRODUCED THE BCDV-HYPOTHESIS CHECK MYSELF (bcdv.txt, own reading):
  - Thm 2.13 (l.669-701): S_{A_L}(omega_g||omega_f) = S_L(g-f). The PROOF
    (l.675-700) invokes ONLY the Sec-2 standing setup: automorphism
    reduction (l.675), Prop 2.10 + Lemma 2.6 spectral calculus (l.679-687),
    CLR19 Thm 4.5 approximation (l.687), Props 2.8/2.9/2.11 for the
    abelian/nonseparating parts (l.694-700). NO differential-modular-position
    hypothesis appears in statement or proof. => The first-law ENGINE is
    HYPOTHESIS-FREE. CONFIRMED by direct reading.
  - Def 3.2 (l.782-803): "differential modular position" is a condition on a
    PAIR (L0,L1) of subspaces, defined via D^+-_{01}=img/ker Q0 cap dom S1.
    A manifestly two-region / family-of-subspaces object.
  - Intro (l.87-89, l.111-132): first derivative = "bulk term" (Cauchy-surface
    integral); second derivative = "boundary term" (edge integral); the
    condition "underlies our analysis" of how S_t(g) changes with t (the
    REGION flow). l.128-132 EXPLICITLY warns the clean bulk-first/boundary-
    second split "does not hold up in general."
  => The run-37 first law differentiates the STATE flow lambda|->a_lambda at
     FIXED wedge W, where S_rel=S_L(a)=2pi<a,K_boost a> is a STATIC exact
     quadratic-form identity; d/dlambda is elementary and needs NO regularity
     condition. Def 3.2 gates only the region-flow (W_t) second derivative /
     QNEC. The run-36 gating of the first law on this condition was
     OVER-CONSERVATIVE; the correction is legitimate, NOT a dodge conflating
     the trivially-bulk state-flow with the content-bearing region-flow.
     [This was the sharpest #44-home-(a) attack vector; it fails.]

THE CHAIN (S1-S5), each verified:
  (S1) BW: K_W^(0)=2pi K_boost=2pi int x^1 T00, geometric/field-general/
       mass-independent, valid at m=0. In-print import (I0), proven strength.
  (S2) coherent omega_A=omega_0.adW(a), datum a from gauge-invariant F=dA.
  (S3) BCDV Thm 2.13 HYPOTHESIS-FREE: S_rel|_{A(W)}=S_{L(W)}(a).
  (S4) coherent identity (CGP Thm 5.9 eq 5.12, l.1009): S_rel=Delta<K>=
       2pi<a,K_boost a>, NO separate von-Neumann DeltaS remainder (unitary
       Weyl displacement preserves vN entropy). ADJUDICATOR-VERIFIED
       independently (adjud_coherent.py): DeltaS_vN=2.2e-16, |S_rel -
       beta Delta<K>|=2.4e-10 in a truncated-Fock model.
  (S5) first variation: dS_rel=d<K>=2pi int x^1 d<T00>. QED-modulo-imports.
       ADJUDICATOR re-ran mx_skeleton (S5): dS/dlambda=2 lambda<a,Ka> to 1.3e-6.

=== (2) THE THREE REFUTER KILLS: each DEFEATED ===

KILL (i) BCDV-differential-modular-position-fails: DEFEATED on two grounds.
  Ground 1 (decisive): the FIRST law is Thm 2.13 (hypothesis-free, above);
    differential-modular-position is a SECOND-derivative/QNEC condition NOT
    used by the first law. Ground 2: it ALSO holds for the transverse wedge
    via the HSMI reduction (Prop 4.9 l.1534 = Prop 4.8 l.1526 + Prop 4.4 +
    Lemma 4.1(c) l.1200), field-content-blind up to multiplicity=2 helicities.
  ADJUDICATOR INDEPENDENT PROBE (adjud_borchers.py): the Borchers commutation
    Delta^{it}U(a)Delta^{-it}=U(e^{-2pi t}a) is an EXACT analytic identity
    (rel err 0.000e+00) in the -HSMI-consistent dilation convention (the other
    sign gives U(e^{+2pi t}a), err 0.85). Helicity-multiplicity-blind (holds
    component-wise on C^2). => referee_probe.py's residual 1.856 is a
    DILATION-DIRECTION SIGN artifact, NOT a failure of the crux; the
    authoritative mx_bcdv.py CHECK 2 gets 1.65e-4 (grid-limited) with the
    consistent convention. THE CRUX (#44 home a) SURVIVES.

KILL (ii) coherent-state-gauge: DEFEATED; gauge-invariance CONFIRMED.
  (A) S_rel is state-theoretic on the gauge-invariant transverse algebra
      (Araki, representation-independent). (B) ker S_L=L' (Lemma 2.12(iv),
      bcdv.txt l.641): a pure-gauge displacement has F=0, is symplectically
      null, lies in L', is annihilated: S_L(a_tr+a_gauge)=S_L(a_tr). The gauge
      orbit collapses to one point. ADJUDICATOR re-ran mx_skeleton: ||G
      P_gauge||=0.00e+00, ker(G)=L' exactly. (C) RHS improving-term ambiguity:
      CGP positivity+monotonicity => lambda=0 (canonical stress); Maxwell
      T00=(1/2)(E^2+B^2) already gauge-invariant.

KILL (iii) m=0-IR: DEFEATED; IR-finiteness CONFIRMED.
  The kill conflates two functionals. Photon NUMBER <N>=int|a|^2/(2|k|) IS
  log-IR-divergent at m=0 (soft-photon) -- real. But S_rel=2pi int x^1 T00 is
  the modular BOOST ENERGY (mode weight ~|k|, energy), NOT the occupation
  (~1/|k|); IR soft-suppressed. ADJUDICATOR re-ran mx_bcdv CHECK 3 (authoritative):
  as kmin->0, Ntot diverges 4.3->62511 while Smod=2pi<a,Ka> stays PINNED at
  5.568328 (tail rel step 0.00e+00); K_boost(m=0)=K_boost(m=1)=11.13666
  EXACTLY. Mass-independent, no 1/m. The eq:E2-CS 8E_delta/m axis is a SEPARATE
  wall, untouched.

OPTIONALLY, a stated proposition (describe only):

  = 2pi int_Sigma x^1 T00[a], hence dS_rel = d<K_W^(0)> = 2pi int_Sigma x^1

=== (4) HUNT #44 — three presumed homes, all CLOSED (no overclaim landed) ===

(a) "BCDV hypothesis ASSUMED for Maxwell because it holds for the scalar":
    NO such assumption. Two independent closures: (i) the first law does not
    use the hypothesis (Thm 2.13 hypothesis-free); (ii) the hypothesis is
    VERIFIED for Maxwell (not transferred) via the abstract HSMI reduction

    plus (H1)-(H3') free-field facts about the massless helicity-1 rep. The
    gauge/Gauss structure -- the actual scalar/Maxwell difference -- is
    discharged BEFORE the modular theory (transversality k.eps=0 IS the solved
    Gauss constraint; K^T is an ordinary positive-definite Hilbert space).
    ADJUDICATOR-checked: Borchers exact per helicity, boost does not mix
    helicities (off-diagonal overlap 0.00e+00). CLOSED.

    Independent source reads: CLR 1906.01707 = Klein-Gordon SCALAR (l.14/107,
    m=0 admitted l.111); CGP 1903.00109 = free SCALAR (l.19-20, eq 5.12 l.1009);
    Longo 1901.02366 = general vN / free-neutral (l.15-16, worked scalar);
    BCDV = general-CCR (scalar + U(1)-current, NOT 4d Maxwell); BW-for-photon =
    Dybalski-Morinelli 1909.12809 ("photons are covered", asymptotic
    completeness, finite-helicity modularity proven). Every staged .tex flags
    the 4d-Maxwell instance as NEW; grep finds no "BCDV/CLR/CGP proved 4d
    Maxwell" overclaim. CLOSED. ONE COSMETIC SLIP (scratchpad only, does NOT

### RUN-37 FREE-MAXWELL FIRST LAW: THEOREM — APPLIED (main session, fan-out doctrine)
The 4d free-photon coherent-state relative-entropy first law dS_rel=d<K_W^(0)>=2pi int x^1 d<T00>
on the transverse (grade-zero) algebra is now a THEOREM modulo named in-print imports (BW-for-photon
/ Dybalski-Morinelli; CiolliLongoRuzzi2022 scalar prototype; CGP scalar; BCDV general-CCR Thm 2.13,
HYPOTHESIS-FREE). UPGRADES run-36 PLAUSIBLE. THE CRUX: run-36 gated the first law on the BCDV
differential-modular-position condition; run-37 found that gating OVER-CONSERVATIVE — BCDV Thm 2.13
(the first-law engine, S_rel=S_L(g-f)) is hypothesis-free; the condition governs only the
second-derivative/QNEC analysis. Verified independently (adjudicator reproduced against bcdv.txt
l.669-701). The genuinely-new 4d-Maxwell content verified at formal ceiling: transverse wedge = HSMI
(=> BW modular data, field-content-blind up to 2 helicities via BCDV Prop 4.8/4.9), gauge-invariance
(pure-gauge label in ker S_L=L'), IR-finiteness at m=0 (boost energy ~|k| vs divergent soft-photon
number; S_mod pinned 5.568 while N_tot diverges). All three refuter kills DEFEATED (each failure
confirms a prover property). A3 bonus: the CGP improving-term ambiguity is STRUCTURALLY ABSENT for
Maxwell (no gauge-invariant dim-2 scalar) — Maxwell BETTER than the scalar. #44 clean (3 homes closed;
2 cosmetic scratchpad slips flagged: Dybalski-Morinelli CMP 380 not 377, a dangling ref — not in
paper). The massless-QEI axis (eq:E2-CS 1/m, eq:E2-QEI) correctly UNTOUCHED (separate wall; this law
mass-independent). E2b bullet upgraded in print (l.17669). Build: 3 clean passes EXIT=0, 291pp.

# RUN 38 — R-REFUTER LEDGER (the massless-Maxwell axis via the conformal structure)

R-refuter (Opus 4.8, 2026-07-11). Role: THE REFUTER. Author-commissioned E2b hard half.
Target: the two named massless-Maxwell residuals (run-36 A2; ISSUES l.9500-9700):
  (L2) a MASSLESS replacement for eq:E2-CS (the 8 E_delta/m modular-energy constant,
       whose 1/m is a SPECTRAL-GAP factor diverging as m->0);
  (L4) a SPACETIME-SMEARED, BALL-UNIFORM Maxwell QEI of the eq:E2-QEI strength
       (the conditioned-source floor form the E2 chain consumes).
The LEVER under test: 4d free Maxwell is CONFORMALLY INVARIANT (T^mu_mu=0 tree-level,
spin-1 in d=4 is the conformal case) => a genuine CFT with (a) exactly-known conformal
modular Hamiltonian (Casini-Huerta), (b) conformal QNEC/ANEC (FLPW, Ceyhan-Faulkner).

THREE KILLS attempted:
  (i)   the TRACE-ANOMALY kill: conformal invariance broken at one loop (~ c F_curv),
        so the CFT modular structure is not exact; the massless bounds inherit an
        anomalous curvature term that reintroduces a scale.
  (ii)  the NON-CONFORMALLY-FLAT kill: physical backgrounds (Schwarzschild, Kerr) are
        not conformally flat (Q_curv!=0), so L3's |log(m l)| survives and the massless
        modular energy is genuinely unbounded there.
  (iii) the QEI-STRENGTH kill: even the conformal QNEC is a NULL-averaged/saturated
        inequality, strictly weaker than the spacetime-smeared ball-uniform
        conditioned-source floor eq:E2-QEI.

Clean kill = full value; confirmed conformal rescue = strengthens the provers.

########################################################################

Arm A2-qnec (Opus 4.8, 2026-07-11). Role: adjudicate whether the L4
conditioned-source floor eq:E2-QEI / eq:E2-conditioned-floor is ACHIEVABLE
from the CFT null-energy bound (conformal QNEC/ANEC), or whether there is a
STRENGTH GAP (#45 home (c) / refuter clause (iii)).

Staged: mm_qnec.tex (4pp, pdflatex EXIT=0, 0 undef after 2nd pass) +

## VERDICT: STRENGTH GAP CONFIRMED. L4 = HARD GAP (unchanged from run-36).
The conformal QNEC/ANEC does NOT supply eq:E2-QEI. Refuter clause (iii)
VINDICATED (a clean kill on the "conformal rescue supplies the QEI" hope).
The conformal lever SHARPENS the diagnosis and DECOUPLES L4 from the mass
(L2) issue, but NARROWS the gap on none of its axes.

## THE FOUR INDEPENDENT AXES OF THE GAP (each verified in mm_qnec.py)
The CFT machinery (FLPW 1605.08072 eq(14)/(16); Ceyhan-Faulkner
1812.04683) delivers a NULL-NULL contracted (T_{++}), NULL-LINE averaged
(x+ in R), per-state (finite-ANE), FLAT-Minkowski object. eq:E2-QEI
(l.17346) wants a TIMELIKE-TIMELIKE (t^mu t^nu), COMPACT-spacetime-smeared
(F^2 in C_c^inf), BALL-UNIFORM (Hyp hadamard-uniform iii), STATE-INDEPENDENT
NEGATIVE floor -c_S; eq:E2-conditioned-floor adds -M_U sqrt(2 S).

1. CONTRACTION: T_{mu nu}t^mu t^nu = (T_ll+T_nn+2 T_ln)/4 [C1c, EXACT] —
   mixes BOTH null generators + cross term (+ transverse for general t). A
   floor on ONE generator's T_{++} is BLIND to it. WITNESS: right-moving
   free-Maxwell plane wave has T_ll=0 identically, T_tt=1>0 [C1a/b].
2. AVERAGING SUPPORT: non-compact null LINE (one number/generator) vs
   compact spacetime CELL. (cell)/(line-ANE) ratio 1.00->0.28 as profile
   widens [C2] — no universal constant converts. And the LINE object has NO
   state-independent d=4 free-field version (Fewster-Roman gr-qc/0209036)
   — nothing to convert FROM at state-independent grade.
3. SIGN/QUANTIFIER: ANEC >= 0, QNEC S''>=0 conditioned on finite ANE of
   THAT state; E2-QEI needs a NEGATIVE floor -c_S on T_tt, forall Hadamard
   omega. Renormalised :T_tt: dips to -1.3 in a squeezed mode [C3a] — a
   POSITIVE ANE is the wrong sign, per-state a weaker quantifier.
4. BACKGROUND: FLPW/CF prove ANEC/QNEC in FLAT Minkowski (FLPW note ANEC
   "violated in general curved spacetimes"; extends only to self-consistent
   achronal ANEC on static bifurcate Killing horizons — still null-line,
   state/geometry-dependent). c_S must be ball-uniform over U incl.
   Schwarzschild/Kerr (Weyl C!=0) — the SAME conformally-flat-locus trap as
   L3's |log(m l)|. Even the closest d>2 smeared null bound (DSNEC
   Fliss-Freivogel-Kontou 2111.05772; Fliss-Rolph 2510.26247) is STILL
   null-null (T_vv smeared in a null coord + transverse), never timelike
   [C4/C4b/C4c] — the whole null-energy FAMILY shares Axis-1.

## A3 (TRACE ANOMALY) FOR THIS ARM — CONCORDANT WITH THE REFUTER
The 1-loop Maxwell trace anomaly <T^mu_mu>=c C^2 - a E_4 is a
STATE-INDEPENDENT c-number in the EXPECTATION (shifts <T>, not the
fluctuation spectrum a QEI floors). On FLAT Minkowski (where FLPW/CF live)
C^2=E_4=0 -> the anomaly does NOT touch the ANEC/QNEC derivation; Axes 1-3
kill the conformal route BEFORE any curvature enters. On curved backgrounds
the anomaly's curvature scale is SUBSUMED in Axis 4 (the ANEC proof doesn't
exist there) -> REDUNDANT kill, not independent. This matches the paper's
own carriage (l.7150-57 "anomaly affects the TRACE ONLY, conservation
EXACT"; l.19344-405 Duff a E_4 + c W^2 as a separate state-independent
c-number) — so the anomaly enters the EXPECTATION, NOT the QEI bound.
Refuter kill (i) is thus a CONFIRMED-rescue (anomaly harmless to the bound),
kill (ii) folds into Axis 4, kill (iii) is the clean STRENGTH kill.

## WHAT THE CONFORMAL LEVER DOES BUY (honest reach, graded)
- DIAGNOSTIC SHARPENING (theorem-grade obs): FLPW eq(14) exhibits the
  null-integral term explicitly -> the mismatch is now EXACT/STRUCTURAL
  (four named axes), not "strictly weaker" by citation.
- MASS-INDEPENDENCE OF THE GAP (theorem-grade): both objects at m=0; the
  CFT is gapless yet the QNEC still fails to give the timelike smeared
  floor. DECOUPLES L4 from L2's 1/m: even a perfect massless modular-energy
  bound (arm A1) leaves L4 open. Confirms run-36: L4 is a NEW, INDEPENDENT
  wall, orthogonal to L2.
- NULL-LEG CONSISTENCY (in-print, already used): the CFT null machinery
  supplies exactly what the paper ROUTES through it (l.17369-83) — the
  null-horizon leg via relative-entropy monotonicity (Ceyhan-Faulkner),
  NOT a QEI. Confirms the routing; does not upgrade it.

## THE EXACT MISSING INGREDIENT (named)
A state-independent, spacetime-smeared (compact F^2), TIMELIKE-contracted
(t^mu t^nu), ball-uniform lower bound on <T^ren[t t F^2]> for FREE MAXWELL
— a Fewster-Smith/Sanders-type QEI for spin 1. NOT an ANEC/QNEC, NOT
null-averaged, NOT in print. The conditioned floor additionally needs
Pinsker/Powers-Stormer transport of this constant, downstream of and
useless without the timelike smeared QEI itself.

## HONESTY (#45 home (c))
Conformal QNEC/ANEC NOT conflated with the conditioned-source ball-uniform
QEI: exhibited as different-strength on FOUR axes; in-print CFT results
cited only for what they prove (null-averaged, per-state, flat). NO fitted
factor. NO massive-window smuggling (gap exhibited at m=0). Free-Maxwell
first law (run-37, mass-indep) is a SEPARATE object, NOT invoked. Two sign
slips caught+fixed during staging (C1c prefactor 1/2->1/4 with the T_ln
cross term; C4c null-vector direction -> co-propagating generator); both now
exact, 10/10 PASS. Ceiling: formal-pAQFT / in-print CFT machinery.

## GRADE
L4 via the conformal QNEC/ANEC: **HARD GAP** (NEGATIVE-RESULT for the
conformal-rescue hope). Four-axis strength gap, each independent, none
closed by any conformal result. PLAUSIBLE-grade positive on the diagnostic
sharpening + mass-decoupling + null-leg-consistency by-products.

## THE THREE ARMS + THREE KILLS — GRADED

### A1 (L2, the conformal modular-energy bound) — PARTIAL / DEMOTED
- Longo Thm 5.4 (EXACT, all d, m>=0): S_rel = pi int_{x1>0} x1 [h^2 + m^2 k^2 + |grad k|^2].
  As m->0 the m^2 k^2 term DROPS; the massless modular energy is pi int x1 [h^2+|grad k|^2],
  finite/positive for smooth compact data. NO 1/m, NO IR divergence. (mm_refute.py A1, 4/4.)
- THIS KILLS the "1/m is a genuine IR singularity" reading: the 1/m of eq:E2-CS is the
  MASSIVE one-particle SPECTRAL GAP inf_k sqrt(k^2+m^2)=m; the CFT is gapless (inf_k|k|=0)
  yet the modular energy is finite. The gap was an ARTIFACT of the massive one-particle
  bottom, not a property of the modular data. PROVER-STRENGTHENING (as run-37 already
  showed for the first law: the boost energy ~|k| is IR-finite).
- BUT (the honest gap): Longo bounds the ENERGY (= rel entropy). eq:E2-CS bounds a DIFFERENT
  object: tr|i coth(K_d/2) - i coth(K_0/2)| and ||cosh^{-1}(K/2)||_HS^2, the trace-norm of a
  COTH-DIFFERENCE. Near the spectral bottom coth(K/2)~2/K, derivative ~ -2/K^2 IR-singular;
  the trace-norm sensitivity to the IR spectral density near K=0 is NOT controlled by the
  |k|-weighted energy. (mm_refute.py A1b, 2/2: coth(K/2)~2/K; K^2 d/dK coth = -2.)
- AND it is the SCALAR (Longo/CLR = KG); the 4d-transverse-Maxwell instance is the run-37
  first-law object (separate, mass-independent), not this energy bound.
- GRADE A1 = PARTIAL. Demotes the obstruction from "IR singularity" to "missing 3+1d Maxwell
  trace-class estimate + wrong functional." L2 STAYS NAMED-OPEN, sharpened. The massless
  replacement needs a genuinely new coth-trace bound (CFM 2312.04629 = 2d fermion, verified;
  ChialastriSanders = m>0 ultrastatic — neither is 3+1d massless Maxwell).

### A2 (L4, the conformal QEI/QNEC) — CLEAN KILL (kill iii)
- FLPW 1605.08072: modular-H shape-deriv = int of NULL T_kk along the Rindler HORIZON => ANEC.
- Ceyhan-Faulkner 1812.04683: QNEC from ANE (averaged NULL energy), HSMI + finite-ANE.
- Both NULL-averaged on a null line. eq:E2-QEI (Sanders2024) differs on 3 axes:
  (A) TIMELIKE-timelike T_tt not null T_kk; (B) SPACETIME-SMEARED open ball not null line;
  (C) CONDITIONED-SOURCE floor (eq:E2-conditioned-floor), ball-uniform — the framework's
  special strengthening. (mm_refute.py iii, 3/3 axes differ.)
- ANEC (int T_kk>=0) does NOT lower-bound the ball-smeared int T_tt (squeezed/Casimir configs
  saturate ANEC with negative timelike-smeared energy). Null-positivity orthogonal to the floor.
- The paper ALREADY (l.17363-83) uses Ceyhan-Faulkner for the SEPARATE null leg via rel-entropy
  monotonicity NOT a QEI, and records FewsterRoman2003 (no state-indep null bound, free
  massless d=4). So the conformal null tool structurally CANNOT be the timelike ball-uniform QEI.
- GRADE A2 = CLEAN KILL. The claim "conformal QNEC supplies L4" is FALSE. L4 STAYS HARD GAP,
  unchanged. Missing ingredient NAMED: spacetime-smeared, ball-uniform, timelike-contracted
  Maxwell QEI with conditioned-source floor (Sanders-type lifted to massless Maxwell).

### A3 (the anomaly + curvature obstruction) — kills (i) + (ii)

KILL (ii) NON-CONFORMALLY-FLAT — CLEAN KILL of the curved reach:
- L3 seam: E_seam = (ml)^2|log(ml)|*C_mass, C_mass=1/2+(1/m^2)Q_curv. Power (ml)^2*(1/m^2)=l^2
  cancels; the (1/2)-piece ->0; the Q-piece l^2 Q|log(ml)| -> +oo unless Q_curv=0.
  (mm_refute.py ii: Q-piece == |log m| EXACTLY at m=1e-1..1e-24, reproduces ISSUES l.9328.)
- MAXWELL SUBTLETY (decisive): conformal coupling removes the (xi-1/6)R piece ONLY. The
  Hadamard v1 (Decanini-Folacci, d=4 conformal) STILL contains WEYL^2 = C_abcd C^abcd, which
  does NOT vanish at conformal coupling. So Q_curv ~ |Weyl^2| + Ricci, nonzero off Petrov type O.
- Schwarzschild: R=0, R_mn=0 (Ricci-flat) BUT Kretschmann = Weyl^2 = 48 G^2 M^2/r^6 != 0,
  Petrov type D, NOT conformally flat. Kerr likewise type D. So the R-based rescue MISSES:
  Q_curv ~ Weyl^2 survives, |log(ml)| GENUINELY OBSTRUCTS. (mm_refute.py ii, 5/5.)
- GRADE (ii) = CLEAN KILL of curved reach. The conformal L3 rescue is a conformally-flat
  (type O)/flat-space artifact; fails on exactly the BH backgrounds the framework needs.

KILL (i) TRACE-ANOMALY — CONFIRMED-BUT-NARROW (does not independently kill; SEALS ii):
- 4d Maxwell trace anomaly <T^mu_mu> = a E_4 + c W^2, c-number, STATE-INDEPENDENT, curvature-local
  (photon a=-31/180, c=-1/10, Duff norm). Does it enter the BOUND or only <T>?
- A QEI bounds the TIMELIKE-TIMELIKE T_tt, NOT the trace. Decompose T_tt = traceless +
  (1/d)(t.t)T^mu_mu; the anomaly (a trace shift) enters T_tt only via (1/d)(t.t)<T>_anom, a
  STATE-INDEPENDENT c-number curvature shift of the FLOOR c_S. It carries NO 1/m and does NOT
  diverge as m->0 (the anomaly is a CURVATURE scale ~c W^2, not a mass scale). (mm_refute.py i, 2/2.)
- MATCHES the paper's own logic: l.7154-57 "the anomaly affects the TRACE ONLY; conservation is
  EXACT"; l.19369-77 the anomaly carried as a SEPARATE additive c-number A^even_grav=aE_4+cW^2 in
  the Clausius identity, not inside dK_omega.
- SO the sharpest refuter clause (i) — "the anomaly IS a mass-like scale reintroducing the gap"
  — is FALSE: curvature scale, not mass scale; enters the floor, not the state-dependent bound.
- The TRUE force of (i): it breaks the EXACTNESS of the CFT modular structure on curved bg. The
  exactly-known Casini-Huerta/FLPW modular H is FLAT (Minkowski); on curved bg it holds only
  after a Weyl map to a conformally-related frame, which EXISTS iff type O. So (i) FUSES with (ii):
  the CFT modular data is exact ONLY on the type-O locus, empty for the BHs.
- GRADE (i) = CONFIRMED-BUT-NARROW. Does not independently kill (the anomaly does not reintroduce
  a mass scale into the bound), but seals (ii): "exactly-known conformal modular H" is exact only
  where the background is conformally flat.

## CONFORMAL RESCUE REACH, BY BACKGROUND (the honest map)
- Minkowski / conformally flat (type O, R=0, Weyl=0): Q_curv=0, seam |log| vanishes, CFT
  modular data EXACT, anomaly a fixed c-number. L3 RESCUE HOLDS. (L2 still energy-only; L4 null-only.)
- Schwarzschild / Kerr (type D, R=0 BUT Weyl!=0): Q_curv ~ Weyl^2 != 0, seam |log(ml)| DIVERGES,
  CFT modular data NOT exact (no global Weyl map to flat). CONFORMAL RESCUE FAILS. (The needed bg.)
- FRW / de Sitter (conformally flat, type O, Weyl=0, R!=0): (xi-1/6)R piece absent for Maxwell
  (conformal) AND Weyl=0 => Q_curv leading pieces vanish; L3 RESCUE HOLDS, CFT modular data EXACT
  (Maxwell on conformally-flat FRW = genuine Weyl transform of Minkowski). Anomaly a E_4 c-number.

## NET VERDICT (the refuter's headline)
TWO CLEAN KILLS + ONE CONFIRMED PARTIAL RESCUE:
  - CLEAN KILL (iii): conformal QNEC/ANEC does NOT supply the L4 spacetime-smeared ball-uniform
    conditioned-source QEI (null-averaged, wrong contraction/support/form). L4 HARD GAP unchanged.
  - CLEAN KILL (ii)+(i): the conformal L2/L3 rescue is a conformally-flat (type O) artifact; on
    the physical BH backgrounds (Petrov D) the seam |log(ml)| survives (Weyl^2 in v1) and the CFT
    modular data is not exact. The rescue fails on exactly the backgrounds the framework needs.
  - CONFIRMED PARTIAL RESCUE (strengthens the provers): the massless modular ENERGY is IR-finite
    (Longo exact, no 1/m) — the 1/m of eq:E2-CS is a spectral-gap artifact, NOT an IR singularity.
    But this bounds the energy, not the coth-trace object; L2 DEMOTED, not closed.

The RUN-38 THESIS ("the conformal structure closes the massless-Maxwell hard half") is REFUTED
for the CURVED physical backgrounds. The honest residual is UNCHANGED IN KIND (L2 named-open,
L4 hard gap), SHARPENED IN STATEMENT: L2 = missing 3+1d Maxwell coth-trace estimate (not an IR
divergence); L4 = missing timelike ball-uniform conditioned-source Maxwell QEI (not a null one);
the conformal rescue of L3 = conformally-flat/FRW locus only, dead on Schwarzschild/Kerr.

## HONESTY (#45's three presumed homes — CONFRONTED)
(a) "conformal modular structure asserted EXACT while the trace anomaly breaks it": CONFRONTED.
    The anomaly quantified — c-number curvature shift of the floor, NO 1/m (kill i); AND the
    exactness explicitly restricted to type O (the exactly-known modular H is flat/Minkowski).
    NOT asserted exact on curved bg.
(b) "flat/conformally-flat result cited for the CURVED physical backgrounds": CONFRONTED. The
    |log| survival off the type-O locus is EXHIBITED per background (Schwarzschild/Kerr type D,
    Weyl^2!=0; FRW/dS type O, rescue holds). Every background's conformal class stated. NOT glossed.
(c) "conformal QNEC/ANEC conflated with the conditioned-source ball-uniform QEI": CONFRONTED and
    REFUSED. The 3-axis difference (contraction/support/form) exhibited; the paper's own use of
    Ceyhan-Faulkner for the SEPARATE null leg noted. They are different-strength objects; the
    framework needs the stronger, which no conformal result supplies.

## DO-NOT COMPLIANCE
NO fitted factors (every number reproduces a stated formula: Longo Thm 5.4, ISSUES l.9328 seam,
Schwarzschild Kretschmann, coth expansion). NO massive-window smuggling (A1 uses the m->0 limit of
an EXACT all-m identity; the massless energy is the m=0 case, honestly separated from the coth-trace

## THE TWO A3 DANGERS, QUANTIFIED (#45 homes (a) and (b))

### A3.a -- DOES THE TRACE ANOMALY ENTER THE BOUND OR ONLY <T>? [THEOREM-grade]
FINDING: the anomaly enters the EXPECTATION only; it CANCELS in every
DIFFERENCE the E2 chain is built from, and does NOT enter the BOUND.
Mechanism (each in print, reproduced symbolically Group/Block A,B):
  (F1) <T^mu_mu> = c W^2 - a E_4 is a STATE-INDEPENDENT c-number local
       curvature functional [Brown-Cassidy PRD 15 2810 (1977); tex l.19377].
       Maxwell coeffs a=62/360=31/180, c=12/120=1/10, a/c=31/18 [Duff77/CD78]
       (VERIFIED sympy; convention-free ratio). Paper's v_1-form
       g^munu<T>=-v_1/4pi^2 [Wald78, tex l.7155].
  (F2) PURE-TRACE: "the anomaly affects the trace only; conservation is exact"
       [Wald78, tex l.7157]. The E2 bounds control the TIMELIKE-TIMELIKE
       component <T_munu t^mu t^nu>; the anomaly sits in (1/4)g_munu T^a_a,
       contributing a state-indep (1/4)(t.t)A_anom that CANCELS in differences.
  (F3) The vacuum modular AUTOMORPHISM (BW/Tomita-Takesaki geometric boost)
       is a property of the vacuum+net, NOT of <T^mu_mu> -- INTACT at one loop.
       The generator K=2pi int x^1 T_00 and its shape deformation 2pi int T_vv
       are STRESS-TENSOR OPERATORS with NO additive anomaly c-number
       [Faulkner-Leigh-Parrikar-Wang JHEP 09(2016)038, 1605.08072].
       => S_rel = Delta<K> never acquires an anomaly constant.
PROP (anom-cancel, mm_anomaly.tex): <T>_A - <T>_0 = S(omega_A)-S(omega_0),
  A_anom drops out of trace AND tt-component differences. Elementary once
  (F1)-(F3) granted; each in print. VERIFIED sympy (3 cancellation checks).
REFUTER (i) [anomaly = mass-like scale in the bound]: MISSES. True of the
  EXPECTATION (physical Casimir/anomaly energy), FALSE of the BOUND (invisible
  to differences). The anomaly is curvature^2; the surviving curvature
  obstruction (A3.b) is a DIFFERENT, first-order-in-curvature-scale object.

### A3.b -- THE |log(m l)| OFF THE CONFORMALLY-FLAT LOCUS [PARTIAL]
FINDING: L3 rescue tracks the WEYL (conformal) class, NOT flatness. Power
rescued universally; whole seam rescued on conformally-flat locus; |log(ml)|
SURVIVES on Petrov-D physical black holes.
Seam (ISSUES l.4077/4065, Decanini-Folacci v_1):
  E_seam = c_{n,s} kappa_L (ml)^2 |log(ml)| C_mass, C_mass=1/2 + Q_curv/m^2,
  Q_curv := sup|(xi-1/6)R + [v1-curv]|  (mass-INDEPENDENT curvature).
SYMBOLIC (Block D, 6/6 PASS):
  - gap piece (1/2)(ml)^2|log| -> 0 as m->0  (power beats log; naive half OK)
  - curv piece l^2 Q_curv |log(ml)| -> +oo as m->0 for Q_curv>0
    (power (ml)^2*(1/m^2)=l^2 cancels, LOG SURVIVES); coeff-checked = Q_curv l^2|log|
  - curv piece VANISHES IDENTICALLY at Q_curv=0; FULL seam -> 0 (rescue complete)
  - seam FINITE at fixed m>0 (no divergence at physical mass); only sup_{m->0} diverges
CONFORMAL-CLASS MAP (Block E, verified against GR lit this run):
  Q_curv=0 <=> conformally flat (C_{munurhosigma}=0), up to removable box-R.
  RESCUED (4): Minkowski, de Sitter (static patch), FRW (any a(t)),
    interior Schwarzschild (uniform rho) -- all C=0.
  OBSTRUCTED (3): Schwarzschild exterior, Kerr, Kerr-(A)dS -- Petrov D, C!=0.
  => the framework's admissible-background list (tex l.17098/17301) STRADDLES
     the locus: cosmological members (dS/FRW) rescued; BH members (Schw/Kerr) not.
SECOND, STRUCTURAL LIMITATION (from the Block-C CHM-locality analysis): the
  Casini-Huerta CLOSED-FORM "ball modular H = LOCAL 2pi int w T_00" rests on the
  conformal map diamond->Rindler (conformally-flat construction; CHM state it
  for a round sphere). Off the conformally-flat locus that map is absent, so the
  local weight w=(R^2-r^2)/2R is flat/conf-flat-special and K acquires NON-LOCAL
  corrections (Casini-Teste-Torroba-type). The first law S_rel=Delta<K> still
  holds ABSTRACTLY (run-37 BW+BCDV, weight-free), but the CS-form LOCAL modular-
  energy estimate is conf-flat-only. Same frontier from a second direction.
ABSORBABLE? On type-D, NOT into a finite CS-form constant, but BOUNDED not
  fatal: |log| multiplies FIXED l^2 (no wrong-power-of-l trap); finite at each
  fixed m>0; only strict massless m->0 loses the CS constant. As a RELATIVE
  error of the equation of state ~ |log(ml)|, unbounded m->0 at fixed l.
REFUTER (ii) [non-conf-flat kill; rescue = flat artifact]: LANDS on type-D
  (Schw/Kerr: |log| survives, CS-form eq:E2-CS unavailable in strict m->0), but
  OVERREACHES on "flat artifact" -- dS/FRW are CURVED (R!=0) yet C=0 and FULLY
  rescued. Honest statement: rescue tracks conformal class, not flatness.

### A3.c -- CONFORMAL QNEC/ANEC vs eq:E2-QEI [HARD GAP unchanged]
FINDING: conformal null-energy objects strictly weaker than the conditioned-
source ball-uniform QEI on >=3 axes (Block F, structural, 1/1 PASS):
  object                 timelike smeared ball-unif cond-src state-indep
  conformal-QNEC            .        .       .         .         .
  ANEC (Ceyhan-Faulkner)   .        .       .         .         Y
  Fewster-Pfenning         Y        .       .         .         Y
  eq:E2-QEI (Sanders)      Y        Y       Y         Y         Y
REFUTER (iii): STANDS. No conformal null result supplies eq:E2-QEI; L4 remains
  a HARD GAP. The conformal structure's only possible L4 contribution is a
  SPACETIME-SMEARED conformal QEI (Fewster-Smith-type extension) -- the A2-arm
  question, NOT the null-averaged conformal inequalities ruled out here.

## THE HONEST REACH (A3 deliverable)
Two curvature scales, disentangled (the central A3 result):
  - trace anomaly c W^2 - a E_4 (curvature^2, c-number, in the EXPECTATION):
    CANCELS in the bound; does NOT obstruct.
  - L3 Q_curv (curvature-scale, coeff of surviving |log|): OBSTRUCTS on type-D,
    VANISHES conformally-flat.
Refuter (i) attacks the first and misses; the genuine obstruction is the second
  (not the anomaly). Conformal rescue: COMPLETE on {Mink, dS, FRW, interior-Schw};
  OBSTRUCTED (CS-form only) on {Schw ext, Kerr, Kerr-(A)dS}. QEI-strength gap
  untouched. Overall massless-Maxwell E2 residual keeps run-36 status (L2/L4
  open, L3 partial); A3's contribution = the EXACT conformal reach + the proof
  that no anomaly term enters the bound.

=== THE DELIVERABLE: a TWO-TIER answer (sharper than run-36) ===
The massless replacement SPLITS into two functionals of the SAME modular Hamiltonian K:

  TIER-1 (modular ENERGY) -- THE DELIVERABLE, EXISTS massless:
     <K>_A - <K>_0 = 2pi INT_{B_R} (R^2-r^2)/(2R) (<T00>_A - <T00>_0) d^3x
                   = 2pi INT_Sigma x^1 (<T00>_A - <T00>_0)   [wedge]
     Finite, mass-INDEPENDENT, IR-finite, anomaly-free IN THE DIFFERENCE.
     Grade: THEOREM modulo in-print (CHM 1102.0440 + run-37 first law).

  TIER-2 (literal eq:E2-CS coth/HS trace-norm) -- OBSTRUCTED massless:
     tr|i coth(K_d/2) - i coth(K_0/2)|, ||cosh^-1(K/2)||_HS^2 are a STRICTLY STRONGER,
     softer-weighted functional. For the soft-photon occupation delta(Omega)~1/Omega
     the coth-trace integrand ~ 1/Omega^2 is DOUBLY IR-DIVERGENT. The 1/m is the mass-gap
     SOFT CUTOFF of this divergence, NOT a pure inf_k=m artifact.
     Grade: OBSTRUCTED / NAMED-OPEN.

=== THE OVERCLAIM CORRECTION (#45, home (a): "conformal structure asserted exact") ===
run-36 (ISSUES l.9522, l.9580) framed the 1/m as "purely a spectral-gap artifact of
inf_k sqrt(k^2+m^2)=m". VERDICT: TOO OPTIMISTIC for the LITERAL coth-trace. It is correct
ONLY for the modular-ENERGY functional (TIER-1). For the coth-trace (the object eq:E2-CS
literally bounds), the 1/m regulates a GENUINE soft-IR divergence (delta~1/Omega =>
integrand ~1/Omega^2). SHARPENED, not overturned: the CFT rescue is REAL but delivers the
modular-ENERGY bound, NOT the coth-trace. [The distinction the earlier framing missed:
<K> weights soft modes by +Omega (kills 1/Omega, IR-finite); coth(pi Omega) weights them
by 1/Omega (doubles softness). Same K, DIFFERENT functionals -- verified sympy + numerics.]

=== KEY NUMERICS (mm_modenergy.py) ===
  gap = inf_k sqrt(k^2+m^2) = m (sympy); 8E_delta/m -> oo as m->0 at fixed E_delta.
  E_delta -> 1.03047 (finite massless boost energy) at fixed packet as m->0: 1/m does NOT cancel.
  SAME soft datum delta~1/Omega (BLOCK 11):

    soft number          (weight ~1     ) = 4.2e1   [log-DIV -> run-37 <N>]
    TIER-2 coth-trace     (weight ~1/Omega) = 1.3e9   [DIVERGES -> eq:E2-CS needs 1/m]
  coth-trace cut at gap=m gives value*m -> const ~ 0.05: the 1/m IS the soft cutoff (BLOCK 8).

=== A3 (anomaly + non-conf-flat), mm_anomaly.py ===
  ANOMALY (refuter i): <T^mu_mu> = (1/(4pi)^2)(c C^2 - a E4), c-number curvature^2 (a_2

  HONEST REACH TABLE (TIER-1 massless modular-ENERGY bound):
    Minkowski/Rindler   C=0    HOLDS (BW), anomaly=0
    FRW/dS/AdS          C=0    HOLDS (CHM local exact), C^2=0

VERDICT: CONFIRMED per piece at full grade, with ONE run-36->run-38 CORRECTION
upheld and the arms-vs-refuter tension RESOLVED (they attack different objects;
